title: Report: Delay Costs 4.3x Higher Than Cost of Capital
h1: Cardiff Releases Industry Report Revealing Cost of Delay Exceeds Cost of Capital by 4.3x
metaDescription: Cardiff Releases Industry Report Revealing Cost of Delay Exceeds Cost of Capital by 4.3x on Cardiff.co.
excerpt: Cardiff has released a new report revealing that delays in accessing funding are now costing small businesses more than four times the cost of the capital itself. The study emphasizes that the time it takes to secure fin

blocks.text: Cardiff has released a new report revealing that delays in accessing funding are now costing small businesses more than four times the cost of the capital itself. The study emphasizes that the time it takes to secure financing can have a more significant impact on business outcomes than interest rates or borrowing costs. These findings highlight the hidden economic drag created when businesses cannot act quickly on opportunities, invest in operations, or respond to market changes. For today’s businesses, speed and accessibility of capital are critical to sustaining growth and competitiveness.

The analysis also points to broader trends affecting small businesses. Even modest delays in receiving funds can lead to missed sales, postponed expansion, and operational strain. Cardiff’s research shows that businesses facing these delays often struggle to maintain momentum, particularly in sectors where timing is essential to securing contracts or managing inventory. The report paints a clear picture. Fast access to funding is a strategic necessity that directly affects a company’s ability to thrive in a competitive environment.

Cardiff’s approach to funding small businesses directly addresses the issues highlighted in the report. By offering fast, flexible lending solutions, the firm helps small businesses secure the capital they need without unnecessary waiting periods. Founder William Stern stated that eliminating delays in funding allows owners to focus on growth rather than administrative hurdles, preserving both opportunities and operational stability.

This report reinforces Cardiff’s commitment to helping small businesses navigate economic pressures efficiently. By prioritizing timely financing, Cardiff ensures that entrepreneurs are not hindered by delays and can act decisively to grow and strengthen their competitive positions. In doing so, Cardiff acts as a strategic partner in supporting the sustained growth and resilience of small businesses across the nation.
